key_insight: Transition-metal substitution turns Ga2O2 from a weak gas adsorber into a tunable platform for selective room-temperature sensing or permanent toxic-gas scavenging.
engineering_relevance: This work points to atomically engineered 2D materials that can be tailored for specific industrial and environmental gas-monitoring or detoxification tasks.
method: DFT calculations on Ga2O2 monolayers substituted with Pd, Zn, Zr, Mo, Ag, Ti, and Pt
Formation/binding energies, structural distortion, and molecular dynamics were used to assess stability
Gas adsorption and selectivity were evaluated against eight toxic industrial gases and three environmental gases
evidence_signal: The abstract reports negative formation and binding energies, negligible lattice distortion, stable molecular dynamics behavior, and selectivity/conductivity changes including up to eight orders of magnitude for Ag-substituted Ga2O2.
watch_item: DFT predictions may overestimate practical sensor performance, so experimental validation under real humidity, mixtures, and device conditions is still needed.
paper_title: Transition-Metal Tailored $Ga_{2}O_{2}$ Monolayer: From Room-Temperature Gas Sensing to Chemical Scavenging
